answer:
verdict: very worried. this is existential-level platform risk. yt changes its DOM constantly (a/b tests, polymer updates, per-user experiments) & has been redesigning aggressively. ur acquisition engine can break overnight w/ 0 warning + no recourse. also chrome web store policy risk on top

how bad exactly:
- yt ships DOM changes weekly, often to random % of users 1st, so breakage looks like "works 4 some users" = hell to debug
- MV3 migration alrdy killed/hobbled lots of extensions, review delays on cws mean even after u fix, days b4 users get patch
- if yt decides ur scraping/injecting violates ToS, google can pull u from cws entirely = 80% of growth gone permanently

what 2 do, in order:

1. defensive coding (this wk)
- stop relying on class names/ids, use multiple fallback selectors + semantic anchors (aria labels, structural patterns)
- wrap every DOM touch in try/catch w/ graceful degradation, never hard-crash
- add telemetry: ping when selectors fail so u kno w/in hrs not days when yt breaks u

2. fast patch pipeline (this month)
- remote config: fetch selectors from ur server so u can fix breakage w/o cws review (allowed if selectors are config, not remote code - dont ship remote code, thats a ban)
- canary tests: headless browser hitting yt every few hrs checking ur selectors still match, alert on fail
- keep cws acct in gd standing, respond fast to policy emails

3. diversify (this quarter - the real fix)
- 80% from 1 fragile channel = fix the concentration, not just the fragility
- push extension users to create accts / email capture asap so breakage doesnt = losing them
- build direct channels: seo, product-led sharing, partnerships, firefox/edge ports (small but hedges cws risk)
- check if yt data api covers any of what u scrape - official api = stable, tho quotas r stingy
- goal: get extension to <50% of new users w/in 6-12 mo

4. worst-case plan
- write the runbook now: if extension dies 4 2 wks, whats the msg to users, whats the fallback product, whats runway impact

bottom line: not panic, but treat it like known tech debt w/ a deadline u cant see. hardening buys u months, diversification is the actual answer
